title: Apparatus and methods for launching electromagnetic waves along a transmission medium
abstract: Aspects of the subject disclosure may include, launching, by a plurality of launchers of a waveguide system, a wave mode that propagates along a transmission medium without requiring an electrical return path, detecting, by the waveguide system, that the wave mode has a propagation loss caused by an obstruction, and generating, by the plurality of launchers, an adjusted wave mode having an electric field structure that reduces the propagation loss of the obstruction. Other embodiments are disclosed.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: adjusting a rotatable mechanism to position a plurality of launchers of a waveguide system in a first azimuthal orientation about an outer surface of a transmission medium, wherein the rotatable mechanism facilitates the plurality of launchers to be placed at different azimuthal orientations about the outer surface of the transmission medium; launching, by the plurality of launchers of the waveguide system positioned in the first azimuthal orientation, an electromagnetic wave guided by the transmission medium, wherein the plurality of launchers generate signals at different phases to produce the electromagnetic wave having a hybrid wave mode, and wherein an electric field pattern of the electromagnetic wave is aligned with the first azimuthal orientation; detecting, by the waveguide system, a propagation loss in the electromagnetic wave that exceeds a threshold, wherein the propagation loss is based on an obstruction disposed on the outer surface of the transmission medium; and azimuthally adjusting, by the plurality of launchers, the electric field pattern of the electromagnetic wave to generate a first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode of the electromagnetic wave having a first adjusted electric field pattern that reduces the propagation loss caused by the obstruction, wherein the first azimuthal orientation of the plurality of launchers is not modified during the azimuthally adjusting of the electric field pattern of the electromagnetic wave. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the adjusting comprises adjusting a phase of each of the plurality of launchers to place the first adjusted electric field pattern of the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode in accordance with an azimuthal orientation that avoids at least in part the obstruction. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the adjusting comprises: determining a second azimuthal orientation of the obstruction; and adjusting a phase of each of the plurality of launchers to place the first adjusted electric field pattern of the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode in accordance with a third azimuthal orientation that avoids at least in part the obstruction at the second azimuthal orientation. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the adjusting comprises offsetting a phase setting of each of the plurality of launchers by a quarter cycle to cause a continual azimuthal rotation of the first adjusted electric field pattern of the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode as the electromagnetic wave propagates along the transmission medium, wherein the continual azimuthal rotation reduces instances when the first adjusted electric field pattern traverses the obstruction. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the adjusting further comprises: obtaining interference information associated with the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode; determining, according to the interference information, a second azimuthal orientation of the obstruction; and adjusting the phase setting of each of the plurality of launchers to generate a second adjusted electromagnetic wave mode of the electromagnetic wave having a second adjusted electric field pattern and a third azimuthal orientation that avoids at least in part the obstruction in the second azimuthal orientation. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the interference information is obtained from a recipient waveguide system that processes the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the interference information is obtained by receiving a reflected signal associated with the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the detecting comprises receiving interference information from a recipient waveguide system that processes the electromagnetic wave. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the detecting comprises receiving a reflected signal associated with the electromagnetic wave. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode comprises an adjusted hybrid electromagnetic wave mode. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of launchers comprises a plurality of dielectric couplers or a plurality of microwave transmitters. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the hybrid wave mode comprises an ÎÎÎ·Î¹,Î· electromagnetic wave mode or an ÎÎÎ·Î¹,Î· electromagnetic wave mode, wherein m > 0, and wherein n >0. 13. A waveguide system, comprising: a plurality of launchers; a rotatable mechanism that enables the plurality of launchers to be placed at different azimuthal orientations relative to an outer surface of a transmission medium; a processing system including a processor; and a memory that stores executable instructions that, when executed by the processing system, facilitate performance of operations, the operations comprising: launching, by the plurality of launchers positioned in a first azimuthal orientation, an electromagnetic wave guided by the transmission medium, wherein the plurality of launchers generate signals at different phases to produce the electromagnetic wave having a hybrid wave mode, and wherein an electric field pattern of the electromagnetic wave is aligned with the first azimuthal orientation; detecting a propagation loss in the electromagnetic wave that exceeds a threshold, wherein the propagation loss is based on an obstruction disposed on the outer surface of the transmission medium; and azimuthally adjusting, by the plurality of launchers, the electric field pattern of the electromagnetic wave to generate a first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode of the electromagnetic wave having a first adjusted electric field pattern that reduces the propagation loss caused by the obstruction, wherein the first azimuthal orientation of the plurality of launchers is not modified during the azimuthally adjusting of the electric field pattern of the electromagnetic wave. 14. The waveguide system of claim 13, wherein the adjusting comprises adjusting a phase of each of the plurality of launchers to place the first adjusted electric field pattern of the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode in an azimuthal orientation that avoids at least in part an obstruction causing the propagation loss. 15. The waveguide system of claim 13, wherein the adjusting comprises offsetting a phase setting of each of the plurality of launchers by a quarter cycle to cause a continual azimuthal rotation of the first adjusted electric field pattern of the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode as the first adjusted electromagnetic wave mode propagates along the transmission medium, wherein the continual azimuthal rotation reduces instances when the first adjusted electric field pattern traverses an obstruction causing the propagation loss.
